post.description: Buy shares (with dry_run support for cost calculation)

cost_sats.description: Total estimated cost in satoshis (LMSR cost + trading fee)

trading_fee_sats.description: Trading fee that goes to market author

post.description: Create a new prediction market

beta.description:
Advanced: LMSR liquidity parameter controlling price sensitivity.
Higher beta = more liquid = smaller price moves per trade.
Mutually exclusive with initial_liquidity - specify one or the other.

category_txids.description:
Txid(s) for categorical dimensions in hex format - required when using [[...]] notation
Each txid must be a ClaimCategorySlots transaction

dimensions.description:
Dimension specification in bracket notation.
Examples: "[004008]", "[004008,004009]", "[[004008,004009]]"

initial_liquidity.description:
Initial liquidity in satoshis to fund the market (recommended).
Beta is derived: β = liquidity / ln(num_outcomes)
Mutually exclusive with beta - specify one or the other.

residual_names.description:
Names for residual outcomes in categorical dimensions (one per [[...]])
e.g., ["Bengals"] when explicit slots are Steelers/Ravens/Browns

post.description: Remove a tx from the mempool
Set the wallet seed from a mnemonic seed phrase
Get total sidechain wealth in sats

Sign an arbitrary message with the secret key for the specified address

All category slots are binary (is_scaled=false) by design.

dst.description: Domain seperation tag for signing messages

post.description: Query votes with filters (by voter, decision, or period)

vote_value.description:
The vote value in real units (e.g., 270 for electoral votes).
For scaled decisions, this is the denormalized value within the
decision's min/max bounds. For binary decisions, this is 0.0 or 1.0.

post.description: Get full voting period information (null period_id = current)
Register as a voter
Submit one or more votes (batch)

items.description: A single vote in a batch submission.

vote_value.description:
The vote value in real units (e.g., 270 for electoral votes).
For scaled decisions with min/max bounds, enter the actual value
within those bounds. The system handles internal normalization.
For binary decisions, use 0.0 (No) or 1.0 (Yes).

question.description: Human readable question for voters (<1000 bytes)

ConsensusResults.description: Results from the SVD consensus algorithm for a voting period.

outcomes.description:
Final consensus outcomes for each decision (slot_id_hex -> value).
For scaled decisions, values are in real units (e.g., 270 electoral votes).
For binary decisions, values are 0.0 or 1.0.

Advanced: LMSR liquidity parameter controlling price sensitivity.

oneOf.description: Claim a decision slot

is_standard.description: Whether it is a Standard slot or not

option_0_label.description: Custom label for option 0 (binary decisions). Defaults to "No".

option_1_label.description: Custom label for option 1 (binary decisions). Defaults to "Yes".

question.description: Human readable question for voters (<1000 bytes)

oneOf.description:
Create a prediction market using dimension bracket notation

Dimension notation examples:
- Single binary: `[004008]`
- Multiple independent: `[004008,004009]`
- Categorical (mutually exclusive): `[[004008,004009,00400a]]`
- Mixed dimensions: `[004008,[004009,00400a]]`

CreateMarket.description:
Create a prediction market using dimension bracket notation

Dimension notation examples:
- Single binary: `[004008]`
- Multiple independent: `[004008,004009]`
- Categorical (mutually exclusive): `[[004008,004009,00400a]]`
- Mixed dimensions: `[004008,[004009,00400a]]`

b.description: LMSR beta parameter (required - liquidity calculated as b * ln(num_states))

category_txids.description:
Txid(s) for categorical dimensions - required when using [[...]] notation
Each txid must be a ClaimCategorySlots transaction

residual_names.description:
Names for residual outcomes in categorical dimensions (one per [[...]])
e.g., ["Bengals"] for AFC North when explicit slots are Steelers/Ravens/Browns

oneOf.description:
Trade shares in a prediction market (buy or sell)
Sign of shares determines direction: positive = buy, negative = sell

Trade.description:
Trade shares in a prediction market (buy or sell)
Sign of shares determines direction: positive = buy, negative = sell

limit_sats.description: Slippage limit: max total cost for buy, min net proceeds for sell (in satoshis)

market_id.description: Market ID standardized across all transaction types per Bitcoin Hivemind specifications

outcome_index.description: Outcome index to trade shares for

shares.description: Number of whole shares to trade (positive = buy, negative = sell)

trader.description: Trader address (required for sell ownership validation)

oneOf.description: Submit a vote for a decision in the current voting period

SubmitVote.description: Submit a vote for a decision in the current voting period

slot_id_bytes.description: 3 byte slot ID of the decision being voted on

vote_value.description: The vote value (0.0-1.0 for binary, scaled range for scaled decisions)

voting_period.description: The voting period this vote belongs to

oneOf.description: Register as a voter (one-time setup)
Submit multiple votes efficiently

voting_period.description: The voting period these votes belong to

oneOf.description:
Claim multiple decision slots as a category (atomic)
The txid becomes the implicit category identifier
NOTE: All category slots are binary (is_scaled=false) by design

ClaimCategorySlots.description:
Claim multiple decision slots as a category (atomic)
The txid becomes the implicit category identifier
NOTE: All category slots are binary (is_scaled=false) by design

VoteBatchItem.description: A single vote in a batch submission.

vote_value.description:
The vote value in real units (e.g., 270 for electoral votes).
For scaled decisions with min/max bounds, enter the actual value
within those bounds. The system handles internal normalization.
For binary decisions, use 0.0 (No) or 1.0 (Yes).

For scaled decisions, this is the denormalized value within the
decision's min/max bounds. For binary decisions, this is 0.0 or 1.0.
